[Project README: /home/ishanp/Documents/GitHub/igs-rust-mcp/README.md]
# IGS MCP Server (Rust)

[![GitHub](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-ishan--parihar/igs--rust--mcp-181717?logo=github)](https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-rust-mcp)
[![GitLab](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitLab-ishan--parihar/igs--rust--mcp-FC6D26?logo=gitlab)](https://gitlab.com/ishan-parihar/igs-rust-mcp)
[![Crates.io](https://img.shields.io/crates/v/rmcp?label=rmcp)](https://crates.io/crates/rmcp)

Intelligence Gathering System - Rust implementation using [rmcp](https://crates.io/crates/rmcp) (modelcontextprotocol/rust-sdk) and [TOON](https://lib.rs/crates/toon-format) for token-efficient AI agent output.

## Overview

IGS MCP monitors intelligence from 223+ curated RSS/HTTP sources across global news, geopolitics, tech, research, and regional topics. Built in Rust for performance and low memory footprint (~14 MB binary, ~5 MB RSS).

### Features

| Domain       | Tools                                                                                | What it does                                                                                                                                      |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **News**     | `news.fetch`, `news.testSource`, `news.enrich`                                       | Fetch from 200+ RSS/HTML/JSON sources with pool/country/city/domain/keyword/time filtering. Offline NLP enrichment (topics, entities, sentiment). |
| **Pools**    | `pools.list`, `pools.upsert`, `pools.delete`                                         | Manage monitoring pool categories                                                                                                                 |
| **Sources**  | `sources.list/upsert/delete`, `sources.autodiscover`, `sources.enableGenericScraper` | Manage RSS/HTTP sources with autodiscovery                                                                                                        |
| **Geo**      | `sources.countries`, `sources.cities`, `sources.domains`                             | List countries/cities/domains with available source counts                                                                                        |
| **Parsers**  | `parsers.list`                                                                       | List available parser ids                                                                                                                         |
| **Reddit**   | `reddit.search`                                                                      | Search Reddit posts and comments                                                                                                                  |
| **Research** | `research.search`, `research.paper`, `research.download`                             | Search arXiv + Semantic Scholar, get paper details, download PDFs                                                                                 |
| **Web**      | `web.search`, `web.scrape`, `web.map`                                                | Web search (Tavily/Firecrawl), scrape, sitemap discovery                                                                                          |
| **Insights** | `insights.*` (6 tools)                                                               | Cross-domain entity connection engine, trending detection                                                                                         |

### Token-Efficient Output

Large data sets are formatted using [TOON (Token-Oriented Object Notation)](https://lib.rs/crates/toon-format), a compact alternative to JSON that reduces token usage for AI agent consumption by ~40–60%.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- Rust 1.75+
- (Optional) Tavily or Firecrawl API keys for web search/scrape

### Build & Run

```bash
cargo build --release
./target/release/igs-mcp
```

### Claude Desktop Configuration

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "igs-mcp": {
      "command": "/absolute/path/to/igs-rust-mcp/target/release/igs-mcp"
    }
  }
}
```

### Environment Variables

| Variable         | Default              | Description                        |
| ---------------- | -------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| `IGS_CONFIG_DIR` | `~/.config/igs-mcp/` | Config directory override          |
| `RUST_LOG`       | `info`               | Log level (e.g., `debug`, `trace`) |

### Config Files

Config YAML files are auto-bootstrapped from `./config/` to `~/.config/igs-mcp/` on first run:

| File            | Purpose                                       |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `settings.yml`  | HTTP client, cache, Tavily/Firecrawl API keys |
| `pools.yml`     | Pool definitions and categories               |
| `sources.yml`   | 223+ RSS/HTTP/JSON source definitions         |
| `countries.yml` | Country metadata for geo-filtering            |

### Web Search Setup

Edit `~/.config/igs-mcp/settings.yml` to add API keys:

```yaml
tavily:
  enabled: true
  api_key: "<YOUR_TAVILY_API_KEY>"

firecrawl:
  enabled: true
  api_key: "<YOUR_FIRECRAWL_API_KEY>"
```

## Architecture

```
src/
├── main.rs      ── Entry point (tokio + rmcp stdio transport)
├── lib.rs       ── Module declarations
├── server.rs    ── MCP server handler + 29 #[tool] methods + TOON helpers
├── config.rs    ── YAML config loading (pools, sources, settings, countries)
├── types.rs     ── Shared types (Pool, Source, NewsItem, ResearchPaper, etc.)
├── http.rs      ── HTTP client with retry + semaphore concurrency + feed cache
├── cache.rs     ── File-based feed cache + query cache with TTL
└── parsers.rs   ── RSS/Atom, JSON Feed, generic HTML parsers + keyword/time filters
```

## Docker

```bash
# Build with Docker
docker build -t igs-rust-mcp .

# Run
docker run -v ~/.config/igs-mcp:/root/.config/igs-mcp igs-rust-mcp
```

Multi-stage Dockerfile: `rust:1.85-slim-bookworm` builder → `debian:bookworm-slim` runtime. Binary stripped with LTO. Final image ~15–20 MB.

---

## Key Design Decisions

1. **rmcp over raw JSON-RPC** — Uses the official modelcontextprotocol/rust-sdk for type-safe tool definitions via `#[tool]` macros.
2. **TOON output format** — Custom token-efficient notation reduces AI agent token consumption by 40–60% compared to raw JSON.
3. **File-based feed cache** — Caches RSS/Atom/JSON feed responses with configurable TTL to avoid redundant HTTP fetches on repeat queries.
4. **Semaphore-concurrency HTTP** — Limits concurrent outgoing requests via tokio semaphore to avoid overwhelming source servers.
5. **Offline NLP enrichment** — Extracts topics, entities, and sentiment from fetched content without external API calls using pattern matching and keyword extraction.

## Size & Performance

| Metric            | Value                                    |
| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| Binary            | ~14 MB (debug), ~7 MB (release-stripped) |
| RSS (idle)        | ~5 MB                                    |
| Docker image      | ~15–20 MB                                |
| Sources monitored | 223+ global RSS/HTTP sources             |
| Startup           | < 100 ms                                 |

## License

MIT

state.title: config/settings.yml

state.output:
<path>/home/ishanp/Documents/GitHub/igs-rust-mcp/src/config.rs</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: use crate::types::*;
2: use anyhow::{Context, Result};
3: use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
4: use tokio::fs;
5: 
6: /// Determine the user config directory.
7: /// Precedence:
8: /// 1. env IGS_CONFIG_DIR
9: /// 2. $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/igs-mcp or ~/.config/igs-mcp
10: pub fn user_config_dir() -> PathBuf {
11:     if let Ok(dir) = std::env::var("IGS_CONFIG_DIR") {
12:         if !dir.trim().is_empty() {
13:             return PathBuf::from(dir);
14:         }
15:     }
16:     let home = std::env::var("HOME").unwrap_or_else(|_| "/tmp".to_string());
17:     let xdg = std::env::var("XDG_CONFIG_HOME")
18:         .unwrap_or_else(|_| format!("{}/.config", home));
19:     PathBuf::from(xdg).join("igs-mcp")
20: }
21: 
22: /// Resolve the package config directory (where default config files ship).
23: pub fn package_config_dir() -> PathBuf {
24:     // Resolve relative to the executable or CWD
25:     let cwd = std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_default();
26:     cwd.join("config")
27: }
28: 
29: async fn file_exists(p: &Path) -> bool {
30:     fs::metadata(p).await.is_ok()
31: }
32: 
33: async fn ensure_bootstrapped() -> Result<()> {
34:     let user_dir = user_config_dir();
35:     let pkg_dir = package_config_dir();
36:     fs::create_dir_all(&user_dir).await?;
37: 
38:     for f in &["pools.yml", "sources.yml", "settings.yml", "countries.yml"] {
39:         let target = user_dir.join(f);
40:         if !file_exists(&target).await {
41:             let src = pkg_dir.join(f);
42:             if file_exists(&src).await {
43:                 let content = fs::read(&src).await?;
44:                 fs::write(&target, &content).await?;
45:                 tracing::info!("Bootstrapped {} from package config", f);
46:             }
47:         }
48:     }
49:     Ok(())
50: }
51: 
52: async fn read_yaml<T: serde::de::DeserializeOwned>(file: &Path) -> Result<T> {
53:     let raw = fs::read_to_string(file)
54:         .await
55:         .with_context(|| format!("Failed to read {}", file.display()))?;
56:     let doc: T = serde_yaml::from_str(&raw)
57:         .with_context(|| format!("Failed to parse {}", file.display()))?;
58:     Ok(doc)
59: }
60: 
61: async fn write_yaml<T: serde::Serialize>(file: &Path, data: &T) -> Result<()> {
62:     if let Some(parent) = file.parent() {
63:         fs::create_dir_all(parent).await?;
64:     }
65:     let txt = serde_yaml::to_string(data)?;
66:     fs::write(file, txt.as_bytes()).await?;
67:     Ok(())
68: }
69: 
70: async fn merge_missing_default_sources() -> Result<()> {
71:     let user_file = user_config_dir().join("sources.yml");
72:     let default_file = package_config_dir().join("sources.yml");
73:     if !file_exists(&user_file).await || !file_exists(&default_file).await {
74:         return Ok(());
75:     }
76: 
77:     let user_doc: SourcesFile = read_yaml(&user_file).await?;
78:     let default_doc: SourcesFile = read_yaml(&default_file).await?;
79: 
80:     let user_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> =
81:         user_doc.sources.iter().map(|s| s.id.clone()).collect();
82: 
83:     let mut merged = user_doc.sources.clone();
84:     let mut changed = false;
85: 
86:     for src in &default_doc.sources {
87:         if !user_ids.contains(&src.id) {
88:             merged.push(src.clone());
89:             changed = true;
90:         }
91:     }
92: 
93:     if changed {
94:         let merged_file = SourcesFile { sources: merged };
95:         write_yaml(&user_file, &merged_file).await?;
96:     }
97:     Ok(())
98: }
99: 
100: pub async fn load_pools() -> Result<PoolsFile> {
101:     ensure_bootstrapped().await?;
102:     let file = user_config_dir().join("pools.yml");
103:     let parsed: PoolsFile = read_yaml(&file).await?;
104:     Ok(parsed)
105: }
106: 
107: pub async fn save_pools(data: &PoolsFile) -> Result<()> {
108:     let file = user_config_dir().join("pools.yml");
109:     write_yaml(&file, data).await?;
110:     Ok(())
111: }
112: 
113: pub async fn load_sources() -> Result<SourcesFile> {
114:     ensure_bootstrapped().await?;
115:     merge_missing_default_sources().await?;
116:     let file = user_config_dir().join("sources.yml");
117:     let parsed: SourcesFile = read_yaml(&file).await?;
118:     Ok(parsed)
119: }
120: 
121: pub async fn save_sources(data: &SourcesFile) -> Result<()> {
122:     let file = user_config_dir().join("sources.yml");
123:     write_yaml(&file, data).await?;
124:     Ok(())
125: }
126: 
127: pub async fn load_settings() -> Result<Settings> {
128:     ensure_bootstrapped().await?;
129:     let file = user_config_dir().join("settings.yml");
130:     let parsed: Settings = read_yaml(&file).await?;
131:     Ok(parsed)
132: }
133: 
134: pub async fn load_countries() -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
135:     ensure_bootstrapped().await?;
136:     let user_file = user_config_dir().join("countries.yml");
137:     let content = if file_exists(&user_file).await {
138:         fs::read_to_string(&user_file).await?
139:     } else {
140:         let pkg_file = package_config_dir().join("countries.yml");
141:         if file_exists(&pkg_file).await {
142:             fs::read_to_string(&pkg_file).await?
143:         } else {
144:             return Ok(serde_json::json!({"countries": []}));
145:         }
146:     };
147:     let val: serde_json::Value = serde_yaml::from_str(&content)?;
148:     Ok(val)
149: }

(End of file - total 149 lines)
</content>
